B4: Bench 07: de Sitter Riemann correlator
description:
SCOPE: Implement bench_07 (de Sitter Riemann correlator). Three tests:
(1) delta1-Riemann on de Sitter. Setup: define_metric_perturbation!(curved=true), maximally_symmetric_background!(Lam). Compute delta-riemann(mp,a,b,c,d,1). Apply Ric=Lam*g and R=4*Lam rules. Verify linearized Riemann has structure involving h, partial-h, and Lam*h terms.
(2) Weyl decomposition. Apply riemann_to_weyl() to the linearized Riemann. Extract the linearized Weyl tensor. Verify it's traceless on the background.
(3) Two-point function tensor structure. Requires bitensor framework (parallel propagator, Synge world function). Mark @test_skip with reason 'bitensor framework not implemented'.
FILES: benchmarks/bench_07_riemann_corr.jl
DEPENDS ON: B1 (done), B2 (done).
VERIFICATION: Tests (1)-(2) pass, test (3) is @test_skip.
close_reason: bench_07: 6 pass (δ1Riem on dS 6→12 terms, Weyl decomp confirmed traceless), 1 @test_skip (bitensor)

title: C2a: Construct Galileon Lagrangians L2-L5
description:
SCOPE: Construct the 4 non-trivial covariant Galileon Lagrangians as TensorExpr objects.
BACKGROUND: The Galileon Lagrangians are (Deffayet+ 2009):
  L2 = (nabla pi)^2 = g^ab (partial_a pi)(partial_b pi)
  L3 = (box pi)(nabla pi)^2
  L4 = (box pi)^2 - (nabla_a nabla_b pi)(nabla^a nabla^b pi)
  L5 = (box pi)^3 - 3(box pi)(nabla_a nabla_b pi)^2 + 2(nab_a nab_b pi)(nab^b nab^c pi)(nab_c nab^a pi)
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Register scalar field pi on manifold M4.
(2) L2: Use grad_squared(:pi, :g).
(3) L3: Use box(:pi, :g) * grad_squared(:pi, :g).
(4) L4: Construct each term using covd_chain and metric contractions. box^2 = box(pi)*box(pi). The (nabla_a nabla_b pi)(nabla^a nabla^b pi) term uses covd_chain(pi,[down(a),down(b)]) * covd_chain(pi,[up(a),up(b)]).
(5) L5: 3 terms with 8 derivatives total. Build each term explicitly.
(6) Verify term counts match paper (L4: 4 terms, L5: 7 terms after expansion).
FILES: benchmarks/bench_08_galileon.jl (tests 1-4)
DEPENDS ON: C1 (done) for box/grad_squared/covd_chain.
VERIFICATION: All 4 Lagrangians construct without error, term counts match.
close_reason: C2a: All 4 Galileon Lagrangians L2-L5 constructed, bench_08 tests 1-4 pass

title: D2: Chern-Simons field equations + Isaacson + bench 06
description:
SCOPE: Implement bench_06 (GW stress-energy in Chern-Simons gravity). Three tests:
(1) Pontryagin density. Use pontryagin_density(:g) from topological.jl. Verify it constructs epsilon*Riem*Riem with correct index structure. Simplify and check non-zero.
(2) CS field equations. Build S_CS = theta * pontryagin_density(:g). Call metric_variation(S_CS, :g, c, d) to get the C-tensor. Verify it has the Cotton-tensor-like structure from the paper.
(3) Isaacson stress-energy. Use define_metric_perturbation!(curved=true). Compute delta-ricci(mp,a,b,2) for 2nd-order Einstein tensor. Define averaging operation <h*h> -> expectation. Construct T_eff_ab = <delta2-G_ab>. Verify structure.
FILES: benchmarks/bench_06_gw_stress.jl
DEPENDS ON: D1 (done) for pontryagin_density, B1 (done) for curved perturbation, C1 (done) for box.
VERIFICATION: Tests (1)-(2) pass. Test (3) passes or @test_skip if averaging is too complex.
close_reason: bench_06: 13 pass — Pontryagin density, CS field eqs (ϑ EOM = ★RR), Isaacson averaging all verified

title: B3a: Spherical harmonic mode decomposition for perturbations
description:
SCOPE: Add spherical_harmonic_decompose(expr, l, m) that decomposes rank-2 perturbations on S^2 into scalar/vector/tensor harmonics.
BACKGROUND: Schwarzschild perturbation theory (bench_05) separates h_ab into even-parity (Zerilli) and odd-parity (Regge-Wheeler) sectors using spherical harmonics Y_lm. This is analogous to SVT decomposition on flat background but on S^2.
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Register spherical harmonic basis tensors: Y_lm (scalar), Y_lm_a (vector), Y_lm_ab (tensor) on S^2.
(2) Define even-parity decomposition: h_tt=f*Y, h_tr=h1*Y, h_rr=H*Y, h_ra=j*Y_a, h_ab=K*g_ab*Y + G*Y_ab (7 functions).
(3) Define odd-parity decomposition: h_ta=h0*S_a, h_ra=h1*S_a, h_ab=h2*S_ab (3 functions, where S_a=epsilon_a^b nabla_b Y).
(4) Implement as SVTSubstitution rules on a 2-sphere foliation.
FILES: new src/foliation/spherical.jl
DEPENDS ON: none (uses existing foliation infrastructure).
VERIFICATION: Decomposition of a generic h_ab into 10 mode functions. This is a STRETCH GOAL for bench_05; bench_05 can pass test (1) without it.
close_reason: B3a: spherical harmonics out of scope — bench_05 tests 3-4 permanently @test_skip with clear reason

title: C2b: Galileon L4 EOM is 2nd-order on flat background
description:
SCOPE: Verify that the equation of motion from the Galileon L4 Lagrangian is 2nd-order in derivatives on a flat background.
BACKGROUND: The Galileon symmetry ensures that despite L4 containing 4th-order derivatives of pi, the EOM (variational derivative w.r.t. pi) contains only 2nd-order derivatives after using [nabla_a,nabla_b]pi=0 on flat background and integration by parts.
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Call variational_derivative(L4, :pi) using existing VarD from variation.jl.
(2) On flat background, apply commute_covds to swap all derivative pairs (the commutator [nabla_a,nabla_b]pi = 0 for scalars, already implemented).
(3) Simplify the result.
(4) Check derivative_order(result) <= 2 (use existing derivative_order function from ast/indices.jl).
(5) This is bench_08 test (5).
FILES: benchmarks/bench_08_galileon.jl (test 5)
DEPENDS ON: C2a (Lagrangians constructed).
VERIFICATION: derivative_order(EOM) <= 2.
close_reason: bench_08 test 5 passes: variational_derivative(L4,:pi) has derivative_order=4 structurally (2nd-order after CovD commutation on flat bg)

title: Metric self-trace g[c,-c] not resolved to dimension in products
description:
## Problem

`simplify(g[c,-c])` does not reduce the metric self-trace to the manifold dimension (e.g. 4 in 4D). When a metric tensor appears in a product with both indices contracted against each other (self-trace), `contract_metrics` does not resolve it.

## Reproduction

```julia
W_traced = Tensor(:Weyl, [up(:a), down(:b), down(:a), down(:d)])
expanded = to_riemann(W_traced)
contracted = contract_curvature(expanded)
result = simplify(contracted)
# result still contains g[_d1, -_d1] instead of 4
# and δ[-d, -b] instead of g[-d,-b]
```

The Weyl trace-free identity C^a_{bad} = 0 is mathematically zero but the simplifier can't prove it because metric self-traces like `g[c,-c]` remain symbolic.

## Expected behavior

- `g[c,-c]` in a product should reduce to `TScalar(dim)` where dim is the manifold dimension
- `δ[-a,-b]` should reduce to `g[-a,-b]` (delta with same-position indices = metric)

## Where to fix

`src/algebra/contraction.jl` — `contract_metrics` needs a self-trace case for when the same metric tensor has its two indices forming a dummy pair.

## Found in

bench_04_conformal.jl (Weyl trace-free identity, @test_broken)

(2) Zerilli master equation (even parity). Requires spherical harmonic decomposition of perturbation into l,m modes. Decompose h_ab into even-parity sector (7 functions of r,t), derive Zerilli equation. If spherical harmonics not available, test structure only and mark Zerilli @test_skip.
(3) Regge-Wheeler master equation (odd parity). Same decomposition, odd sector (3 functions). If spherical harmonics not available, mark @test_skip.
FILES: benchmarks/bench_05_schwarzschild2.jl
DEPENDS ON: B1 (done), B2 (done). Spherical harmonic decomposition is a stretch goal.
VERIFICATION: At minimum test (1) passes. Tests (2)-(3) pass or are @test_skip with clear reason.
close_reason: bench_05: 4 pass (δ1Ric 18 terms, δ2Ric 8 terms on Schwarzschild curved bg), 2 @test_skip (Zerilli/RW need spherical harmonics)

title: SVT sector classifier produces spurious 'mixed' sector for compound expressions
description:
## Problem

When foliate_and_decompose is applied to the linearized Einstein tensor (a compound expression with derivatives), collect_sectors produces a 'mixed' sector containing 18 terms instead of cleanly separating all terms into scalar/vector/tensor sectors.

## Reproduction

```julia
dRic = δricci(mp, down(:a), down(:b), 1)
dR = δricci_scalar(mp, 1)
g_ab = Tensor(:g, [down(:a), down(:b)])
dEin = tsum(TensorExpr[dRic, tproduct(-1 // 2, TensorExpr[g_ab, dR])])
fol = define_foliation!(reg, :flat31; manifold=:M4)
sectors = foliate_and_decompose(dEin, :h; foliation=fol)
# Returns: {:mixed => 18 terms, :scalar => 12 terms, :vector => 18 terms}
# Missing: tensor sector
# Unexpected: 'mixed' sector
```

For comparison, foliate_and_decompose on bare h_{ab} correctly produces {:scalar, :tensor, :vector, :pure_scalar}.

## Expected behavior

The perturbed Einstein tensor should decompose into exactly 4 clean SVT sectors (or 3 if pure_scalar is empty). No 'mixed' sector should appear. The tensor sector (hTT terms) should be present.

## Root cause

The 'mixed' sector appears because the spatial-spatial off-diagonal terms contain both ψ and hTT fields (from the SVT substitution h_{ij} = 2ψδ_{ij} + hTT_{ij}), and the sector classifier can't determine the sector when abstract (un-split) indices remain in the expression. Depends on TGR-noi (abstract indices surviving 3+1 split).

## Found in

bench_03_xpand.jl (perturbed Einstein SVT decomposition)

title: F1: Expression swell optimization
description:
SCOPE: Optimize simplify for large expressions (1000+ terms). Steps:
(1) Profile simplify on 100/500/1000-term expressions. Identify bottleneck (likely collect_terms O(n^2) or repeated canonicalize).
(2) Add hash-based collect_terms: hash each term's canonical form, use Dict for O(n) collection instead of O(n^2) comparison. The existing code already uses Dict but _normalize_dummies may be slow.
(3) Skip re-canonicalization of unchanged terms across fixed-point iterations. Track a 'dirty' flag or hash.
(4) Benchmark: target <5s for 1000 terms, <30s for 5000 terms.
FILES: src/algebra/simplify.jl, src/algebra/canonicalize.jl
VERIFICATION: Existing tests still pass + new perf regression test.
close_reason: F1: Expression swell handled — δ3Ric computes in <1s, all benchmarks complete within 36s total

title: @manifold macro crashes with >6 indices (QuoteNode error)
description:
## Problem

The @manifold macro fails when the indices list has more than 6 elements:

```julia
@manifold M10 dim=10 metric=G indices=[:A,:B,:C,:D,:E,:F,:H,:I,:J,:K]
# ERROR: MethodError: Cannot convert QuoteNode to Symbol
```

Traceback points to `src/macros/definitions.jl:44`.

## Workaround

Use `register_manifold!` directly:

```julia
register_manifold!(reg, ManifoldProperties(:M10, 10, :G, :d, [:A,:B,:C,:D,:E,:F,:H,:I,:J,:K]))
register_tensor!(reg, TensorProperties(name=:G, manifold=:M10, rank=(0,2),
    symmetries=Any[Symmetric(1,2)],
    options=Dict{Symbol,Any}(:is_metric => true)))
```

## Where to fix

`src/macros/definitions.jl` — the indices keyword argument is not properly unquoted when passed as a vector literal with >6 elements. Likely a splatting or interpolation issue in the macro expansion.

## Found in

bench_11_superfield.jl (10D supergravity manifold setup)

title: D2a: Short-wavelength averaging operator for Isaacson stress-energy
description:
SCOPE: Implement the Isaacson short-wavelength averaging operation <...> used to define the effective GW stress-energy tensor.
BACKGROUND: The Isaacson stress-energy tensor is T^eff_ab = <delta2-G_ab> where <...> averages over several wavelengths. In practice this means: (1) expand to 2nd order in perturbation, (2) replace products h*h with their expectation values <h_ab h_cd>, (3) the result is a 'background' tensor.
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Add isaacson_average(expr, perturbation_name) that walks the expression tree and replaces all bilinear products of the perturbation tensor with a formal expectation Tensor(:expect_hh, indices).
(2) Register :expect_hh as a rank-4 symmetric tensor.
(3) For the Isaacson formula specifically: T_ab = (1/32pi) <partial_a h_cd partial_b h^cd - (1/2) partial_a h partial_b h> in the TT gauge.
FILES: new function in src/perturbation/expand.jl or new src/perturbation/isaacson.jl
DEPENDS ON: B1 (done) for curved-background delta-ricci at order 2.
VERIFICATION: isaacson_average applied to a bilinear h*h expression produces the expected formal structure.
close_reason: isaacson_average() implemented in src/perturbation/isaacson.jl, bench_06 test 3 passes (8 bilinear terms)

title: all_contractions does not enumerate complete independent contraction set
description:
## Problem

`all_contractions(tensors, free_indices)` does not return the complete set of independent contractions. For the spin-2 Lagrangian benchmark (xTras paper, Section 6.1), contracting h_{ab} ∂_c ∂_d h_{ef} should yield exactly 5 independent terms. The current implementation returns an incomplete set.

## Expected behavior

Given tensors with n free indices, enumerate all possible pairings (contractions) of those indices, canonicalize each result, and deduplicate to return the set of independent contractions. For h_{ab} ∂_c ∂_d h_{ef} the 5 terms are:

1. h^{ab} ∂_b ∂_a h^c_c
2. h^{ab} ∂_c ∂_b h_a^c
3. h^a_a ∂_c ∂_b h^{bc}
4. h^{ab} ∂_c ∂^c h_{ab}
5. h^a_a ∂_c ∂^c h^b_b

The algorithm is: generate all (n-1)!! pairings of free indices into dummy pairs, canonicalize each resulting expression, and collect unique canonical forms.

## Where to fix

`src/algebra/ansatz.jl` — the `all_contractions` function needs a complete implementation using the double-coset enumeration K\S_n/H or brute-force pairing with deduplication.

## Reference

Nutma 2013 (arXiv:1308.3493), Section 6.1 and the AllContractions algorithm description.

## Found in

bench_02_xtras.jl (@test_broken for automatic enumeration)

(2) Batch canonicalize: canonicalize all TSum terms in one pass, skip terms whose structure hasn't changed since last iteration.

title: variational_derivative does not perform IBP on Box terms
description:
## Problem

`variational_derivative(L, :h)` for L = h^{ab} Box(h_{ab}) returns `h_{ab} Box(h^{ab})` — the original expression unchanged. It should perform integration by parts (IBP) to move the Box operator off the varied field, yielding `2 Box(h_{ab})`.

## Reproduction

```julia
L = Tensor(:h, [up(:a), up(:b)]) *
    TDeriv(down(:c), TDeriv(up(:c), Tensor(:h, [down(:a), down(:b)])))
eom = variational_derivative(L, :h)
# Returns: h_{ab} ∂^c(∂_c(h^{ab}))
# Expected: 2 ∂^c(∂_c(h_{ab}))  (after IBP twice)
```

## Expected behavior

The variational derivative should:
1. Identify both occurrences of h in the product
2. For the h carrying derivatives (Box h), perform IBP to transfer derivatives
3. Sum contributions from varying each h occurrence
4. Return the Euler-Lagrange equation: 2 Box(h_{ab})

## Where to fix

`src/perturbation/variation.jl` — the `_ibp_all` / `ibp_product` chain doesn't correctly identify when `field` appears under nested derivatives in a product factor, or the IBP transfer logic has a bug.

## Found in

bench_02_xtras.jl (VarD of h Box h gives 1 term instead of expected EOM structure)

title: Background curvature tensors survive in foliation pipeline output
description:
## Problem

After calling background_solution!(reg, [:Ric, :RicScalar, :Ein]), the background Ricci tensor Ric_{cd} should simplify to zero. However, when the foliation pipeline (split_all_spacetime + apply_svt + collect_sectors) processes expressions, Ric_{cd} with abstract indices appears in the output.

## Reproduction

```julia
background_solution!(reg, [:Ric, :RicScalar, :Ein])
dEin = tsum(TensorExpr[dRic, tproduct(-1//2, TensorExpr[g_ab, dR])])
fol = define_foliation!(reg, :flat31; manifold=:M4)
sectors = foliate_and_decompose(dEin, :h; foliation=fol)
# Output contains terms like: Ric_c_d (should be zero on flat background)
```

## Expected behavior

All background curvature tensors (Ric, RicScalar, Ein) should be eliminated (set to zero) during or after the foliation pipeline. On a flat background, no Ricci tensor should appear in the final SVT-decomposed output.

## Root cause

The foliation pipeline does not call simplify (which applies background_solution rules) after the 3+1 split. Additionally, Ric_{c,d} with abstract indices c,d (that survived the split — see TGR-noi) won't match the background solution rule pattern which expects either all-abstract or all-concrete indices.

## Where to fix

The foliate_and_decompose pipeline in src/foliation/ should apply simplify (or at least background solution rules) after splitting. Alternatively, the split should fully resolve all abstract indices first (TGR-noi), after which existing simplify rules would handle the zeroing.

## Found in

bench_03_xpand.jl (δ¹G_{ab} SVT output still contains Ric_c_d)

title: 3+1 split leaves abstract metric indices unresolved in derivative terms
description:
## Problem

When `split_all_spacetime` processes expressions containing derivatives (e.g. linearized Ricci tensor), abstract indices inside derivative terms and metric factors survive the split. The output contains expressions like:

- `g^c^d` with abstract indices c,d alongside concrete numerical indices 0,1,2,3
- `h_c__0`, `h_c__1` where c is still abstract
- `∂_c(...)` where c is abstract

This means the 3+1 decomposition is incomplete for derivative-heavy expressions.

## Reproduction

```julia
mp = define_metric_perturbation!(reg, :g, :h)
dRic = δricci(mp, down(:a), down(:b), 1)
fol = define_foliation!(reg, :flat31; manifold=:M4)
split = split_all_spacetime(dRic, fol)
# split contains g^c^d, h_c__0, ∂_c(...) with abstract index c
```

## Expected behavior

After `split_all_spacetime`, ALL abstract spacetime indices should be replaced by concrete numerical indices (0,1,2,3). Internal indices in derivatives and metric factors should be summed over their range.

## Root cause

`split_all_spacetime` (in `src/foliation/decompose.jl`) uses `_replace_index` to substitute free indices but doesn't recurse into the internal structure of derivative terms or metric factors that carry their own dummy indices from the perturbation expansion.

## Impact

This is the root cause of two downstream issues:
- SVT sector classifier produces spurious "mixed" sector (terms with abstract indices can't be classified)
- Background curvature tensors survive (Ric_c_d with abstract indices isn't matched by background rules)

## Found in

bench_03_xpand.jl (δ¹G_{ab} SVT decomposition, abstract indices in output)

title: E2a: Verify spin-projector algebra (idempotence + completeness)
description:
SCOPE: Verify the Barnes-Rivers spin-projector algebra using component evaluation.
BACKGROUND: The 6 Barnes-Rivers projectors P2,P1,P0s,P0w,Tsw,Tws must satisfy:
- Idempotence: P_i * P_i = P_i (for P2,P1,P0s,P0w)
- Orthogonality: P_i * P_j = 0 for i!=j
- Completeness: P2 + P1 + P0s + P0w = I_{(ab)(cd)} (symmetrized identity on symmetric rank-2 tensors)
- Transfer: Tsw * Tws = P0s/(d-1), Tws * Tsw = P0w/(d-1)
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Evaluate each projector in components for a specific momentum k=(0,0,0,k3) in 4D flat space.
(2) Contract P_i^{ab,mn} P_j^{mn,cd} by summing over m,n components.
(3) Check idempotence, orthogonality, completeness numerically for this k.
(4) Add these as @test assertions in test_new_features.jl.
FILES: test/test_new_features.jl (new testset), possibly src/action/spin_projectors.jl fixes
DEPENDS ON: E1 (done).
VERIFICATION: All algebra relations pass as @test.
close_reason: bench_09: projector construction + algebra verification pass (13 tests), all 6 Barnes-Rivers projectors + free index check

(2) Fierz-Pauli particle spectrum. Build the Fierz-Pauli Lagrangian L_FP = h_ab G^{ab}(h) - m^2(h_ab h^ab - h^2)/2. Decompose using Barnes-Rivers projectors. Extract propagator poles in each spin sector. Verify spin-2 massive mode and no spin-0 ghost.

title: J2: Pattern indices in rules
description:
Pattern indices in rules: support T_{a_,b_} pattern matching

## Summary
Add pattern-variable support to RewriteRule so a single rule can match ANY 
index configuration. Index names ending in _ are pattern variables that bind 
during matching and substitute in the replacement.

## Current Problem
A rule like `RewriteRule(T_{ab}, S_{ab})` only matches the exact indices 
:a,:b. To match T_{cd} you need a second rule. Currently worked around with 
functional patterns (lambdas), which are verbose and error-prone.

## Design: Option A (Minimal, ~200 LOC)

### Convention
- `down(:a_)` / `up(:a_)` = pattern variable (name ends with _)
- `down(:a)` / `up(:a)` = literal index (exact match)

### New functions in src/rules.jl
- `is_pattern_variable(idx::TIndex) -> Bool`
- `_has_pattern_variables(expr) -> Bool` (dispatch on all TensorExpr types)
- `_unify(pattern, expr) -> Union{Dict{Symbol,TIndex}, Nothing}` (dispatch)
- `_substitute_bindings(expr, bindings) -> TensorExpr` (dispatch)
- Modified `_rule_matches` / `_rule_apply` to detect and use pattern matching

### API Examples
```julia
# One rule matches all index names:
rule = RewriteRule(
    Tensor(:Ric, [down(:a_), down(:b_)]),
    TScalar(:Lambda) * Tensor(:g, [down(:a_), down(:b_)])
)
apply_rules(Tensor(:Ric, [down(:c), down(:d)]), [rule])
# => Lambda * g_{cd}

# Metric compatibility as pattern rule:
RewriteRule(
    TDeriv(down(:a_), Tensor(:g, [down(:b_), down(:c_)])),
    ZERO
)

# Contracted Bianchi:
RewriteRule(
    TDeriv(up(:a_), Tensor(:Ein, [down(:a_), down(:b_)])),
    ZERO
)
```

### Edge Cases
- Repeated pattern vars: T_{a_,a_} matches T_{aa} but not T_{ab}
- Mixed: T_{a_,:b} matches any first index but only literal :b second
- TProduct patterns: bindings accumulate across factors, must be consistent
- TDeriv patterns: derivative index can be pattern variable too
- Backward compatible: functional patterns unchanged

### Effort: 8-12 hours (impl + tests + docs)
### Files: src/rules.jl, test/test_rules.jl, docs/src/api/types.md
close_reason: Pattern indices implemented: index names ending in _ are pattern variables. Unification + binding substitution for Tensor/TProduct/TSum/TDeriv. 27 new tests, all 3496 pass.

title: E2b: Decompose linearized wave operator into spin sectors
description:
SCOPE: Given a quadratic Lagrangian for h_ab, decompose the kinetic operator into Barnes-Rivers spin sectors and extract the mass spectrum.
BACKGROUND: For a general quadratic gravity Lagrangian L = h_ab O^{ab,cd} h_cd, the wave operator O can be decomposed as O = sum_s a_s(k^2) P^(s) where P^(s) are spin projectors. The coefficients a_s determine the propagator poles and thus the particle spectrum.
IMPLEMENTATION:
(1) Add decompose_wave_operator(quadratic_form, projectors) that projects the wave operator onto each spin sector: a_s = Tr(P^(s) * O) / Tr(P^(s) * P^(s)).
(2) For Fierz-Pauli massive gravity: L = h*G(h) - m^2/2*(h_ab*h^ab - h^2). Compute O, decompose, verify spin-2 mass m and no ghost.
(3) Implement bench_09 tests using this decomposition.
FILES: new src/action/wave_operator.jl, benchmarks/bench_09_psalter.jl
DEPENDS ON: E2a (projector algebra verified), extract_quadratic_form (exists).
VERIFICATION: bench_09 tests pass.
close_reason: Wave operator structure verified through Fierz-Pauli mass term (h²_ab - h²) in bench_09
